app.title: PathLab
app.heroText: Build walls, compare algorithms, and watch how a search moves from start to target.

onboarding.title: Ready to explore?
onboarding.text: Draw walls on the board first, then start a pathfinding algorithm whenever you are ready.
steps: Click empty cells to draw walls
Choose BFS, DFS, Dijkstra or A*
Press Run visualization and watch the search

status.initial: Draw walls, choose an algorithm, then run the visualization.
status.reset: Grid reset. Ready for a new visualization.

tip.title: Beginner tip
tip.text: Start with BFS. BFS spreads from the start like a water wave, which makes it easy to observe.

bfs.name: BFS
bfs.description: BFS searches in rings or layers around the start. It checks all nearby cells first, then cells that are farther away. On this grid, BFS reliably finds the shortest path.

dfs.name: DFS
dfs.description: DFS goes as far as possible in one direction before backtracking. It can look less organized and does not always find the shortest path, but it clearly shows how depth-first search behaves.

dijkstra.name: Dijkstra
dijkstra.description: Dijkstra always expands the cheapest known cell next. On this grid all moves cost the same, so it looks similar to BFS. It becomes especially important when paths have different costs.

astar.name: A*
astar.description: A* combines the path cost so far with an estimate toward the target. This usually makes A* search more directly than Dijkstra and visit fewer cells.
